subject: Daily Run Report — Sunday, June 21, 2026
body_clean: Mayor's office — 24-hour run summary for Engine 1, Truck 1, Ambulance 35/36, Shift B. Total 19 runs: 13 EMS (4 chest-pain/cardiac, 3 falls, 2 difficulty-breathing, 1 OD with Narcan administered, 3 lift-assists), 4 fire/hazard (1 outside rubbish at 2900 block Washington Blvd, 1 wires-down on Marshall Ave after a tree limb, 2 good-intent), and 2 automatic alarms (Eisenhower Tower, Memorial Park field house — both unfounded, panel reset). Three simultaneous EMS calls at 18:42 required Ambulance 36 recall and one MABAS box transport assist from Melrose Park. Average turnout 1:08. No civilian or firefighter injuries. Apparatus all in service.

subject: Daily Run Report — Monday, June 22, 2026
body_clean: Mayor's office — 24-hour run summary for Shift A. Total 16 runs: 11 EMS (3 falls with transport, 2 chest-pain, 2 diabetic, 1 seizure, 1 MVA with extrication on Mannheim Rd at St. Charles — one patient to Loyola, 2 refusals), 3 fire/hazard (1 working kitchen fire at 519 Bohland Ave, contained to stove, ~$4k loss; 1 CO alarm at 1200 25th Ave, source a failing water heater, ComGas notified; 1 dumpster fire behind 3400 St. Charles Rd), and 2 alarm activations (Roosevelt School, reset; Lincoln Plaza retail, sprinkler trouble signal referred to fire_inspector). Hydrant 25th & Bohland used on the Bohland fire flowed adequately. Average turnout 1:14. No injuries.

subject: Commercial fire inspection — Eisenhower Tower, 1701 Eastern Ave (violations)
body_clean: Mayor's office — annual fire/life-safety inspection completed at Eisenhower Tower office building, 1701 Eastern Ave, on June 16. Five violations cited: (1) blocked exit corridor on 3rd floor, storage in egress path — IFC 2021 Sec. 1031.2; (2) one fire extinguisher overdue for annual service — IFC 906.2 / NFPA 10; (3) sprinkler riser room being used for janitorial storage — NFPA 13; (4) two emergency exit signs not illuminated — IFC 1013.3; (5) fire-alarm panel showing a supervisory trouble light, last inspection tag 14 months old — NFPA 72. Building manager given 21 days to correct. Re-inspection scheduled. Forwarding to fire_chief for the record.

subject: Commercial fire inspection — Route 64 Tavern, 600 St. Charles Rd (violations)
body_clean: Mayor's office — assembly-occupancy inspection at Route 64 Tavern, 600 St. Charles Rd, completed June 17 ahead of summer patio season. Violations: (1) occupant-load sign missing at main room, posted capacity must be reverified — IFC 1004.9; (2) rear exit door fitted with a deadbolt requiring a key from the inside — IFC 1010.2.3, immediate hazard, ordered corrected same day and verified; (3) Type K hood-suppression system last serviced over 8 months ago — NFPA 96 / NFPA 17A requires 6-month intervals; (4) extension cords used as permanent wiring behind the bar — IFC 605.5. Item 2 abated on site. Items 1, 3, 4 due in 14 days. Re-inspection to follow.

subject: Commercial fire inspection — Lincoln Plaza retail strip, 3300 St. Charles Rd
body_clean: Mayor's office — inspection of the Lincoln Plaza multi-tenant retail strip at 3300 St. Charles Rd on June 18. Three violations across two units: (1) Unit C (nail salon) — combustible chemical storage near a hot-water heater with no separation, exceeding allowable quantities — IFC Ch. 50 / 5704; (2) Unit C — sprinkler heads painted over in the back room — NFPA 13, heads must be replaced; (3) Unit F (vacant) — fire-alarm sprinkler trouble signal active, the same trouble reported by fire_watch on the 22nd — NFPA 72. Landlord notified. Corrections due in 14 days. Painted sprinkler heads flagged as priority.

subject: Re-inspection — Eisenhower Tower, 1701 Eastern Ave
body_clean: Mayor's office — re-inspection follow-up on Eisenhower Tower, 1701 Eastern Ave (original inspection June 16). Building management acted quickly: the 3rd-floor egress corridor is now clear (IFC 1031.2 resolved), the overdue extinguisher was serviced and re-tagged (NFPA 10), the riser room is cleared, and both exit signs are now illuminated (IFC 1013.3). One item remains open — the NFPA 72 fire-alarm panel supervisory trouble. The alarm vendor is scheduled for June 25 to replace a failed initiating-circuit module. I'll close the file after that visit. Overall good cooperation. Forwarding to fire_chief.

subject: Working structure fire — 2218 Bohland Ave (NFIRS #2026-00417)
body_clean: Mayor — notifying your office of a working structure fire on June 19 at 2218 Bohland Ave, a 1.5-story single-family home. Dispatched 14:07; first-in Engine 1 reported smoke showing from the second floor at 14:13. Fire was knocked down by 14:41, declared under control 14:58. A MABAS Division 11 Box Alarm brought engines from Melrose Park, Maywood, and Berkeley for water supply and RIT. The two residents self-evacuated and were uninjured; one firefighter treated on scene for heat exhaustion, not transported. Estimated loss $90,000 to structure and contents. The Red Cross is assisting the family. NFIRS report #2026-00417 opened. Cause/origin still under investigation — follow-up to come.

subject: Working structure fire — 2218 Bohland Ave (NFIRS #2026-00417)
body_clean: Chief, thank you for the fast, thorough notification and please pass my gratitude to both shifts and our MABAS partners in Melrose Park, Maywood, and Berkeley — that kind of mutual aid is exactly why the agreement matters. I'm relieved the family and your crews came through safely; please keep me posted on the firefighter treated for heat exhaustion. I'd like to send a note to the residents and connect them with any village resources the Red Cross doesn't cover. When the cause-and-origin work is done, send me the summary so I can brief the Board. Outstanding work.

subject: Cause & origin follow-up — 2218 Bohland Ave (NFIRS #2026-00417)
body_clean: Mayor — cause-and-origin follow-up on the Bohland Ave fire (NFIRS #2026-00417). Our investigator, working with the Cook County deputy fire marshal, traced the area of origin to a second-floor rear bedroom. Cause is classified accidental — a window-unit air conditioner on an overloaded power strip; no accelerants, no indication of anything suspicious. Smoke alarms were present but the second-floor unit had a dead battery, which slowed the family's notification. We're using this to push our free smoke-alarm program door-to-door on that block. The firefighter treated for heat exhaustion is fine and back on duty. File will be finalized this week; I'll have the written summary to you for the Board by Friday.

subject: Hydrant flow-test results — north-end mains, June 18
body_clean: Mayor's office — fire and Public Works completed annual hydrant flow-testing on the north-end grid June 18. Twelve hydrants tested between Washington Blvd and the Eisenhower right-of-way. Eleven flowed within NFPA 291 range; static pressures 62-71 psi, residual acceptable, classed blue/green for available flow. One hydrant at 1300 Marshall Ave flowed low (under 500 GPM) and was tagged out of service — PW is excavating to check for a partially closed auxiliary valve. We've color-coded caps accordingly. Recommend the low hydrant be cleared before the Taste of Bellwood event load on that side of the village. Sharing with fire_chief and pw_director.

subject: MABAS Division 11 mutual-aid — outgoing assist to Maywood, June 20
body_clean: Mayor — for your awareness, Bellwood provided mutual aid under MABAS Division 11 on June 20. Maywood struck a Box Alarm for a commercial fire at a warehouse on 1st Ave; our Truck 1 and four personnel responded on the change-of-quarters/box assignment from roughly 21:30 to 01:15. No Bellwood injuries, no apparatus damage. Our station was backfilled by an Ambulance crew held in quarters and a Berkeley engine moved up to cover. This is the third reciprocal box this quarter — twice we've drawn on the system (including the Bohland fire) and once we've given. The agreement is working as intended. No cost to the village beyond standard overtime, which is within the budgeted line.

subject: Re-inspection — Route 64 Tavern, 600 St. Charles Rd
body_clean: Mayor's office — re-inspection of Route 64 Tavern, 600 St. Charles Rd, this morning (original June 17). Results: occupant-load sign now posted at 96 after recalculation (IFC 1004.9 cleared); Type K hood-suppression system serviced and tagged by a licensed contractor (NFPA 96 / 17A cleared); the behind-bar extension cords were replaced with permanent receptacles by a licensed electrician (IFC 605.5 cleared). The key-locked rear exit deadbolt remains removed and the panic hardware verified (IFC 1010.2.3). All items resolved — the establishment is cleared for the summer patio season. Closing the file. Copying fire_chief.

subject: CO incident summary — 1042 25th Ave, June 15
body_clean: Mayor's office — note on a carbon-monoxide call June 15 at 1042 25th Ave, a single-family residence. Resident reported a sounding CO detector around 22:10. Engine 1 metered 48 ppm at the basement furnace and 22 ppm on the first floor. Occupants were moved to fresh air; one elderly resident evaluated by Ambulance 35, refused transport. We ventilated, shut down the furnace, red-tagged it, and advised ComGas, who responded and confirmed a cracked heat exchanger. No transports. This is a good reminder to keep promoting CO-detector checks; the home's detector worked exactly as it should and likely prevented a tragedy. Forwarding to fire_chief.
